text_clean: Es un hermoso lugar lleno de historia, pero es una lástima que ahora solo se pueda llegar hasta un muro caído y nada más, es lamentable que dentro de la zona arqueológica exista comercios informales, construcciones que no son acordes a la arquitectura del lugar, que ocasionan mucha contaminación visual y también acústica, por la música a volumen alto y por la emisión de humos y olores fuertes en zona frontal de los muros prehispánicos.

Machu Picchu itself is certainly an impressive archeological site. The price (entry ticket per circuit 152S, resp. 50$) is very high in my regards, especially since there is zero information given on the site itself. Further all means of transport are overpriced compared to the rest of Peru and there is no alternative. Overall a nice experience, but felt overpriced, without any reasonable alternative for means of transport, too little/no information and thus a overrated.

It’s an overpopulated tourist trap. Out of the 2,5 hours I was waiting for 2 hours on instagram addicts taking the same selfie everyone else also has. The guide told very basic stories which is not worth your money. You might as well go to Disneyland and stand in line all day. Better take route 4 and hike up Huayna Picchu by yourself. Probably one of the worst ancient temples I’ve visited in Latin America.

Be aware that the price of the ticket and attitude of the object management is awful. Price is much more than for other wonderful sites like collosum, Piza tower, neushwenstein etc. You can pick only one of available circuits. In our (4th) circuit you additionally had to choose between seeing hall of mirrors and temple of kondor... The view of course is beautiful. But imo mountains are better. No information boards available. Toilets, bus service, luggage storage additionally paid

Interesante para conocer más sobre la historia de nuestro Perú. Pude conocer el museo, el cual toma 20-30 minutos de recorrido, y luego la ciudadela. El precio que pagas al ingresar te permite visitar ambos lugares. Sobre el museo, puedes visitarlo con o sin guía (precio adicional). En mi caso recorrí el museo con mi pareja por nuestra cuenta, pero para la ciudadela sí contratamos una guía por un precio adicional (s/ 60). Ojo que la distancia entre el museo y la ciudadela es aprox. entre 5-7 minutos en auto, pero pueden ir caminando, aunque el sol es intenso en enero. Sobre el costo, yo fui con mi pareja, pero pueden juntarse entre más personas y pagar menos si se dividen el costo. Me tocó una guía amena, así que disfrutamos conociendo la historia del lugar. ¡Muy entretenido! Solo es una lástima que aún no se conozca toda la ciudadela, como las tumbas (siguen estudiándola). Aún así, recomiendo visitar porque está cerca del centro de Trujillo.

text_clean: Just like for the most of people, Machu Picchu was on my bucket list, because I started planning the trip a bit late, tickets to actually enter Machu Picchu were sold out already and I did circuit 1 with the famous view from the terrace. It was definitely cool to see, but I have to admit that I was a bit disappointed. The whole trek was very short from start to finish and when you factor all the expenses to actually see it, the crowds, waiting time, it felt a bit flat for the price. Overall Machu Picchu is worth a visit, but it's the definition of a tourist trap. Be sure to plan everything beforehand and you should have a better experience.

This is a park where you can take your time and let it all soak in.

text_clean: Qué pena desdé que ya no está el Dr. Uceda está en un estado de abandono, tal pareciera que el Dr. llevó todo a la tumba, no hay interés de parte del ministerio de la cultura por hacer algo por su conservación, es una lástima que no se valoré la labor tan grande que se hizo con un gran equipo de gente que hicieron posible que se pueda visitar ahora, es importante valorar a nuestras culturas ancestrales, conocer nuestros orígenes, de allí se saca mucha enseñanza, cuanto nos falta aprender realmente, los trabajos que se realizaron allí son invaluables, lo recomiendo.

The archaeological site itself was honestly very impressive and absolutely worth visiting. You can really feel the importance of the place, and the ruins are beautifully preserved. Unfortunately, the guided tour didn’t quite match the quality of the site itself. Our local guide seemed a bit disengaged and gave very limited historical information in English. Most of the tour felt like simply moving from one point to another without much storytelling, interesting context, or those little fun facts that usually make places like this come alive. It wasn’t terrible, just surprisingly flat for such a fascinating location. We actually ended up leaving before the tour finished because it became hard to stay engaged. That said, I would still absolutely recommend visiting the site itself, it’s genuinely impressive and worth seeing. I’d just suggest doing a little reading beforehand to appreciate the history.

Disappointing :-( First of all, my experience has nothing to do with the actual site. The view, ruins, landscape and llamas are surreal! My disappointing experience is about the management of the site. I knew it was a touristy place and quite expensive to go. Most places are restricted/controlled and you can't enter inside the temples etc..you can just go around. Apparently a few years ago everything was open (said some friends who went before). And then you have all the tour groups that take a lot of spaces on the path. I almost didn't take any pictures, I didn't feel I had the freedom to do so because the path goes only on one way (so you can't go back on your path) and people are moving so you kind of feel a bit in a tour group without being in it and you kind of have to go forward. I didn't feel free to walk around/take pictures as I would have liked to. And then there are 4 choices of tickets, with different circuits, bit it doesn't give you access to everywhere with only one ticket, so you need to buy another one. This wasn't very cleared on the website. I just find that you put so much effort (physically, mentally and financially) to get there (waking up super early, doing all the trip to get to Aguas Calientes, walking up the (many!) stairs to the entrance, and the cost etc), in the end to leave with a bitter feeling. Is it worth it? I'm still unsure if it was.
Not worth the journey, view is not spectacular. Too much of a hassle to see a few condors.

Amazing IF you plan your trip. This is a wonderful family outing but you need to prepare some things in advance. Have cash and change with you - from paying your entrance, to paying snacks and bouncy castles and other rides for the kids, you want to have cash with you. The area where the park is located, is bit far from the main tourist area, so take a cab that will leave you right at the entrance. Third tip - look on the website in advance, the times of the water show. Even though you will still enjoy the different fountains around the place, do not miss the water show, it lasts 15 minutes and it is absolutely worth it.... ohh and last tip, do not buy churros there!!! For the rest, super fun, families will enjoy it!!

Visited before it closed down last year but the site is breathtaking. Well worth a visit if you get the opportunity. I know quite a few people are disappointed at not being able to get close or into the site but I've travelled a lot less to see a lot less and been happy (Stonehenge is one that springs to mind). Anyway, it's worth the visit even just to stand next to a site older than the Incas in my view!

Desde la entrada puedes subir a la ciudadela a pie o a caballo. Los únicos baños están en la entrada, así que te recomiendo ir al baño antes de comenzar la caminata

Muy interesante la cultura, hermoso paisaje, los antiguos si que sabían vivir en comunión con la naturaleza, que lastima que no se puede llegar a la misma por carretera porque el teleférico lo impide, eso debió de hacerse de otra manera, mucha gente desea visitar, pero el acceso no es muy recomendable para cierto tipo de discapacidades, los trabajos de investigación y restauración están muy bien, pero hay otras prioridades, la atención de las agencias de viajes que laboran en Chachapoyas lo único que quieren es lucrar, los restaurantes en Tingo nuevo malos, solo hay uno que recomiendo en la plaza de armas de Tingo Nuevo.

Hermosa ciudadela con impresionantes murallas. Es lamentable que por falta de mantenimiento se hayan perdido algunas de las murallas, ahora están tratando de recuperarlas, pero el daño podría ser irrecuperable.

Really interesting site but the lack of information in the museum and in the citadel is a shame. We felt we had to get a guide for 60 soles. destination: Huacas del Sol y de la Luna
text_clean: Incredible! Just amazed at how big a lot of Peruvian cultures were, far from popular believe that only Incas dominated the landscape. Note how good the colors are conservated, through thousand years. One thing that caused an impact was AI-APAEC, a Moche god who could transform into a lot of figures, but the fangs were ever there.

destination: Huacas del Sol y de la Luna
text_clean: Excelente lugar para conocer la historia, lástima que haya sido dañado en la época de la llegada de los españoles..pero ha sido y sigue siendo protegida.. Luego de varios años se está recuperando varios nuevos ambientes y seguro en los próximos años aumentarán lo ambientes a recorrer en la visita...antes de la visita a la Huaca se recorre el museo de sitio y allí mismo en la boletería te programan el recorrido a la Huaca..El.museo impacta por la cantidad de artículos impresionantes...Juan Carlos el guía muy bueno en las explicaciones y respuestas durante el recorrido de 1.45 hrs aproximadamente... recomiendo la visita!!

text_clean: Lugar increíble que te hace sentir la historia en carne viva, es genial imaginar lo que significaban estos templos para la antigua cultura moche, un lugar lleno de vellesa histórica, lástima que sólo se puede visitar el sitio con guía y con un recorrido preestablecido, pero tiempo al tiempo pues los trabajos de imbestigacion en el lugar aún continúan, esperemos que nos rebelen mucho más de esta increible cultura, el centro arqueologico cue ta con los servicios bacicos, si te encuentras por la ciudad no dudes en visitar este increíble lugar, locomoción publica desde y hacia el centro de la ciudad.

destination: Líneas y Geoglifos de Nasca y Palpa
text_clean: A bit disappointing. The panamericana (the highway) ruins quite a few spots and to see all the figures (the lines aren't really that interesting) you need to travel quite a bit. We used just the observation towers and the distance and the haze makes experiencing it quite hard. Obviously getting a small plane to show you the figures is the other alternative, which we didn't do. I was hoping the museum (Maria Reiche) would give us mire insight, but that is more about her life and less about the lines...

This can definitely be missed if you don't have time or care about history. Still very interesting to see how Adobe mud bricks were made and millions of them form this flat top pyramid. The tour isn't that exciting, seems very scripted, don't expect answers to anything beyond the script. Life size figurines recreate scenes of the past. I bet Miraflores was beautiful before the concrete jungle sporuted up and stole the life of the land. Not sure I'm a fan of the animals there, but at least they're alive. Definitely see this Before Machu Picchu.. if you come here after, you'll be disappointed (as the tourists next to me were).
Nice history, but the fact that it's constructed feels disappointing
Nice history, but the fact that it's constructed feels disappointing

The site is definitely worth visiting. It is very impressive. Sadly, we were forced to join a tour instead of walking at our own pace, and the tour felt very rushed. There was never time to just appreciate the site, but instead we had to strictly follow the tour guide or we would miss half of the information. Furthermore, the tour guide's accent and fast pace made it difficult to understand much of their commentary.

Tour guide is limited in explaining the Archeology of the site, the history of human sacrifices that was found at the site. A little bit disappointed, but overall it's an interesting experience.

This place is beautiful and precious, it's a UNESCO World Heritage Site for sure. Unfortunately, there was a bummer, there was no guided tours offered by the museum. There were no information panels on the grounds, except for two or three at the entrance. A map of the site, which we were given upon request, contained virtually no information, but advertisements for other museums in Cusco. For the 70 PEN entrance fee, Guests could expect a bit more effort. You should definitely hire a guide to understand everything that's to discover.

Beautiful and extensive ruins just outside of Cusco city center. Walls are made of beautiful huge boulders fitted perfectly together and the lookout has amazing views of the city. Leila and I walked up here after a walking tour dropped us off not far from the final climb. It was and unexpectedly hot and sunny day, and the altitude still makes climbing hard. But it was worth the climb

El lugar es simplemente espectacular, un 15 de 10 sin duda. La vista, la historia y la energía que se siente ahí son incomparables. Es un sitio que recomendaría visitar al 100%, realmente vale la pena. Sin embargo, tuvimos una mala experiencia en la tienda de recuerdos. Las señoras que atienden se negaron a darnos precios y prácticamente nos ignoraron, mientras que a unos turistas estadounidenses sí los atendieron con toda la amabilidad. A pesar de que mostrábamos interés en comprar, no recibimos ni la más mínima atención. Una pena que un lugar tan increíble se vea opacado por ese tipo de actitudes. Ojalá puedan mejorar ese aspecto, porque el sitio lo merece.

Ojalá puedan mejorar ese aspecto, porque el sitio lo merece.

Si bien no tuvimos la suerte de contar con un buen guia, se puede apreciar el santuario. Llama la atencion, si bien se comprende porque no habia cerca, porque fue construido en barro. Pero hay historia. Lastima que el hombre y la lluvia lo destruyen lentamente...

Merecería 5 estrellas, pero falta información en la taquilla, por lo demás todo fantástico, hubo la suerte de tener una guía muy amable y con conocimiento, es un lugar que invita a repetir la experiencia, por mi parte recomendaría en verano, para poder apreciar mejores vistas
